title: CURRENT SESSION STATE block: per-turn honest enumeration of mode/tools/conversation
current_state: consciousness.txt references a 'CURRENT SESSION STATE block injected at runtime' for mode-specific refusal text, but the dynamic segment carries only timestamp + awareness. The model infers its mode/toolset from conversation cues — the root condition for tool-miming.
target_state: Every turn's dynamic segment includes a 4-line block: mode, conversation_id + turn number, tool count actually available this turn (post-allowlist), and the one-line refusal rule for the current mode ('normie: no tools — if asked to persist, say: type root mode'). Cheap (~40 tokens), removes the model's need to guess its own state.
migration_plan: 1. build_session_state_block(cfg, conversation_id, turn_number, n_tools) in agent/prompt.py.
2. Append in _respond_via_config where prefetch lands (dynamic segment — never the cached static one).
3. Refusal one-liners live in ModeConfig (new field refusal_hint: str).
4. Tests: block present in dynamic segment for each mode; tool count reflects allowlist; static segment unchanged (cache safety).
risk_notes: Must land in the DYNAMIC prompt segment only — touching the static segment breaks the 3-seg prompt cache economics.
